title: Status Lines, Informational Responses, Content Rules, and Response Parsing · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective response control-data and content decision parser; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in response control-data and content decision parser and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse HTTP version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and CRLF while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative.
Process zero or more permitted informational responses before the final response with bounded count and protocol-specific handling.
Parse HEAD response metadata as if GET were selected while never consuming response content bytes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Response Retry Ambiguity
steps.instruction:
Order the Response Retry Ambiguity evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Response Retry Ambiguity.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Response Retry Ambiguity assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Response Retry Ambiguity, keep Response Retry Ambiguity connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A debug protocol team handles case 9233 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound informational responses and transition HEAD, no-content, and CONNECT responses correctly; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify incomplete, malformed, unsolicited, and connection-lost responses without blindly replaying methods whose effects may have occurred.

content.text: Order work so Response Retry Ambiguity has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify incomplete, malformed, unsolicited, and connection-lost responses without blindly replaying methods whose effects may have occurred.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Response Retry Ambiguity, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: No final response means the server certainly did not process the request.
